source: Singapore and Penang Municipalities position of London agents__CO273:669:4
filename: Singapore and Penang Municipalities position of London agents__CO273:669:4.txt
summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** Colonial Office file on the **status, duties, remuneration, and oversight** of the **London agents (Messrs. Peirce & Williams)** for the **Municipalities of Singapore, Penang (Georgetown), and Malacca**, especially after wartime disruption and the transfer of municipal funds to the **Crown Agents for the Colonies**.  
- **Geography/time period:** Concerns **Straits Settlements/Malaya** (Singapore, Penang, Malacca) with administration in **London**; main actions and correspondence span **1942–1943** (with retrospective audit to **1 Jan 1942** and arrangements beginning **1 Jan 1943**).  
- **Major administrative decision:** The **Secretary of State (Colonel Oliver Stanley)** decided that from **1 Jan 1943** the **Crown Agents** would take custody/management of all liquid and invested municipal funds in the UK, while **Peirce & Williams** would continue operational agency work under Powers of Attorney (contracts, claims, staff/dependants payments, Provident Fund matters, and records for ex gratia loan-interest payments).  
- **Audit arrangements:** The question of auditing municipal accounts after takeover by the Crown Agents was settled: the **Colonial Audit Department** agreed to audit the municipal accounts **retrospective to 1 Jan 1942**, though gaps were anticipated due to missing documents and uncertainty in 1942; note that **Peirce & Williams had no records for the Malacca Municipal Provident Fund** (as stated).  
- **Trading with the Enemy (T/E) issue:** The transfer of funds to the Crown Agents initially **overlooked T/E requirements**; the **Trading with the Enemy Department (Treasury/Board of Trade)** regularised the position in **May 1943** by issuing authority under the **Trading with the Enemy Act, 1939** for banks (e.g., **Chartered Bank of India, Australia & China**; **Hongkong & Shanghai Banking Corporation**) to transfer municipal funds/securities to the Crown Agents.  
- **Remuneration of London agents:** Peirce & Williams’ inclusive fee was set at **£3,600 per annum from Singapore** and **£600 per annum from Penang** for a year from **1 Jan 1943**, subject to alteration with **six months’ notice after 30 Jun 1943**; in late **1943** officials discussed whether remuneration was high relative to overheads (audited overheads for year ended **31 Mar 1943: £1,596 0s 3d**) and agreed to **defer any reduction**, considering review for the year beginning **1 Jul 1944** based on audited overheads for year ended **31 Mar 1944**.  
- **Notable numbers/financial details:** Peirce & Williams reported sterling assets (examples) and balances for **1 Jan 1942** and **30 Jun 1943** (e.g., Singapore general account about **£1.07m** in 1942; sinking fund about **£1.85m** in 1942; later balances also listed), plus contract/shipment figures (e.g., Singapore indents outstanding reduced to **£21,863** by **30 Jun 1943**; Penang indents outstanding **£1,427** by **30 Jun 1943**; shipment diversions/loss recoveries itemised).
